tagline: Accidents trail them like a nervous pet.

kitchen.fail: <name> spilled the salt jar into the batch of curry they were making, but never realized it and served it proudly to all the guests.
kitchen.mid: After nearly dropping an entire casserole on the floor, <name> locked in and even managed to get everything out on time. One guest swore there was a subtle hint of brimstone in the potatoes. <name> just smiled.
kitchen.success: Against all odds this brunch slaps. <name> insists their sigilcrafting has really improved lately.

fail.mara: Mara made them stand inside a salt circle all night with their hands above their head and recite the Orphic hymn to Saturn.

salt_casserole.review_callback: I still have heart palpitations from that salt casserole.

housekeeping.fail: <name> knocked over a crystal lamp which caused a minor explosion. Nothing was fully consumed by flame, but the guests don't seem to be taking that well.
housekeeping.mid: Rooms are clean-ish. <name> left a handwritten note on each pillow apologizing for thought crimes.
housekeeping.success: Everything sparkles. <name> also sorted the minibar by emotional resonance. Guests find it upsettingly uplifting without really knowing why.

fail.mara: Mara banned them from the east wing until the smoke clears.

tour_guide.fail: <name> opened the basement door at the same time Mara was making an offering. One guest has not stopped muttering; their friend is threatening to call the police.
tour_guide.mid: <name> went off on an interesting tangent about taoist alchemy and the group never left the living room.
tour_guide.success: <name> forgot three stops but improvised so well a guest asked if the wrong turn was part of the liminal architecture. Vibes are weirdly up.

fail.mara: Mara did not speak to them for the rest of the day, but everyone heard her counting to one thousand in Latin.

tagline: Confuses honesty with hospitality in a truly genuine way.

tour_guide.fail: <name> opened the basement door at the same time Mara was making an offering to the nameless one. One guest has not stopped muttering; their friend is threatening to call the police.
tour_guide.mid: The tour was charming until <name> answered a question about the blood room with complete honesty. Two guests laughed; one went very quiet.
tour_guide.success: <name> forgot three stops but improvised so well a guest asked if the wrong turn was part of the liminal architecture. Vibes are weirdly up.

fail.mara: Mara said honesty is a virtue and also a liability. She assigned them to laundry.

tagline: Desperately wants to be mystical; terrified of being normal.

ritual.fail: A guest questioned whether the ritual was real. <name> overcompensated, tried for an outfit change, and eventually just ran off weakly sobbing.
ritual.mid: The ceremony looked legitimate if your experience of the occult is MAD magazine. Authenticity lands in the middle: theater with heart.
ritual.success: <name> committed so hard it became convincing. They still do not believe in themselves, but the guests absolutely do.

fail.mara: Mara found them behind the barn sobbing and punching a bale of hay. She left them to it.

kitchen.fail: The meal was rosemary arranged into a pentagram and set on fire.
kitchen.mid: <name> plated like a magazine and cooked like a student. Photogenic, beautifully lit, and just barely edible.
kitchen.success: <name> followed a recipe for once despite intense shame for consuming mainstream media. They were more upset when everyone was delighted.

fail.mara: Mara extinguished the pentagram and chided <name> for theatrics.

housekeeping.fail: <name> left a reversed tarot card in every drawer. One was The Tower. A guest interpreted this as a threat and packed early.
housekeeping.mid: Rooms are tidy with small mystical flourishes: salt lines, moon charts on napkins. Clean enough; vibes are complicated.
housekeeping.success: Immaculate and tasteful. <name> added one personalized omen of restful sleep note per room. Guests love the attention.

fail.mara: Mara collected the tarot cards. She kept The Tower for her desk.

tour_guide.fail: <name> spoke only in riddles until a guest asked a direct question about checkout time. They spiraled, sat on a hay bale, and whispered something about manifesting capitalism.
tour_guide.mid: The tour had excellent aesthetic signage. <name> pointed at ordinary objects and called them omens. Most guests nodded politely.
tour_guide.success: <name> found a rhythm: one riddle, one fact, one compliment. A guest tipped them in crystals. They have never felt so seen.

tagline: A special brand of 'yes, and' occultism.

ritual.fail: <name> went full avant-garde: whispering, crawling, smearing themselves with stage blood. It did NOT land.
ritual.mid: Strong stage presence, novice occult mechanics. Looks cool; authenticity is community theater with incense.
ritual.success: The flair carried it. <name> hit every beat like opening night. Guests said it felt expensive, which is close enough.

fail.mara: Mara asked if the blood was fake. <name> said that ruins the magic.

tour_guide.fail: <name> delivered the tour as a one-act tragedy about the parents that never loved them back. Guests applauded at the wrong moment.
tour_guide.mid: Heavy on atmosphere, light on directions. <name> gestured at fog like it was a character. People got lost but looked moved.
tour_guide.success: A master class: <name> made a fence post feel haunted. A guest asked for their autograph on a pamphlet about ley lines.

tagline: Food can be magical, right guys? Right?

kitchen.fail: The cooking was passionate but not particularly good. <name> called it rustic intimacy. The guests called it a situation.
kitchen.mid: <name> cooked with passion and cleaned with indifference. The guests were happy; the next person to use the kitchen will not be.
kitchen.success: A triumph of culinary drama. <name> called it rustic intimacy. Guests asked for seconds and left inspired.

success.mara: Mara ate a full plate in silence. That is her highest praise.

food_praised.review_callback: The food was the only normal thing about this place. I mean that as a compliment. I think.
